summary: Matched fighter audio helper `func_80088148` by replacing its hand-written assembly with C. The function maps a fighter SFX id through `func_80087D0C`, starts playback via `func_800237A8`, stores the returned handle in `Fighter::x2160`, and applies a randomized pitch/parameter through `func_80024B94` only for SFX ids 332 through 370. The PR also tightened related audio prototypes in fighter, item, and lb audio headers to use `enum_t` and signed/int parameter types needed for matching.
what_changed: Removed the assembly implementation of `func_80088148` from `asm/melee/ft/ft_unknown_006.s`.
Added a C implementation of `func_80088148(Fighter* fp, enum_t sfx_id, int sfx_vol, int sfx_pan)` in `src/melee/ft/ft_unknown_006.c`.
Updated the fighter header prototype from `void func_80088148(Fighter*, u32, u32, u32)` to `void func_80088148(Fighter*, enum_t, int, int)`.
Changed item SFX helper prototypes and definitions for `func_8026AE84`, `func_8026AF0C`, and `func_8026AFA0` from `u8 pan, u8 volume` to `int pan, int volume` while retaining `enum_t sfx`.
Updated `func_800237A8` in `lbaudio_ax.h` from anonymous `u32` parameters to `u32 func_800237A8(enum_t sfx_id, u8 sfx_vol, u8 sfx_pan)`.
Added the missing declaration `void func_80024B94(int, int);` to `lbaudio_ax.h`.
smart_moves: Preserved the original assembly control flow in readable C: remap SFX id, call the audio play function, store the handle, then call the post-playback adjustment function.
Used `enum_t` for the SFX id instead of a raw integer type, making the callsite intent clearer without inventing a named SFX enum.

Kept `sfx_vol` and `sfx_pan` as `int` at the fighter wrapper boundary, while declaring `func_800237A8` with `u8` volume/pan parameters to reflect the callee's byte-sized argument behavior from the assembly `clrlwi` operations.
Introduced an inner block with a local `int rand`, which likely helped match stack/register lifetimes while keeping the conditional randomization logic clear.
Encoded the magic SFX-id range check directly as `sfx_id >= 332 && sfx_id <= 370`, matching the assembly comparisons against `0x14c` and `0x172`.
Declared `func_80024B94` in the audio header instead of relying on an implicit or local declaration, improving cross-file type consistency.
decomp_lessons: When matching wrappers around audio APIs, inspect argument narrowing in the assembly. Here `clrlwi` on the volume and pan arguments supported byte-sized callee parameters for `func_800237A8`.
A function's public wrapper prototype may need wider `int` arguments even if the downstream callee consumes `u8`; matching can depend on where truncation occurs.
Replacing an asm function often exposes incorrect neighboring prototypes. Fixing related item/audio prototypes in the same PR can be necessary to keep declarations consistent.
Do not over-name unknown audio functions prematurely. This PR kept address-based names but improved parameter names and types where evidence existed.
For randomization code, preserve exact bounds and arithmetic. `HSD_Randi(200) - 100` corresponds to the original `li r3, 0xc8` and `addi r4, r3, -100`.
naming_conventions: Use `enum_t sfx_id` or `enum_t sfx` for sound-effect identifiers rather than generic `u32` where callsite evidence supports an enum-like id.
Use descriptive parameter names such as `sfx_id`, `sfx_vol`, and `sfx_pan` for audio calls instead of unnamed integer parameters.
Keep unresolved functions in their `func_XXXXXXXX` form when no stronger semantic name is established.
Use `SFX_NONE` as the named constant for `-1` in the audio header, though this PR did not directly use it in the matched function.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The deleted assembly saved `r3` as the `Fighter*`, called `func_80087D0C`, then reused the returned value as the SFX id; the C mirrors this by assigning back to `sfx_id`.
The assembly stored the return from `func_800237A8` at offset `0x2160`; the C expresses this as `fp->x2160 = func_800237A8(...)`.
The assembly compared the remapped SFX id against decimal range 332 to 370 (`0x14c` to `0x172`) before calling `HSD_Randi(200) - 100`; otherwise it passed zero.
The final call `func_80024B94(fp->x2160, rand)` matches the assembly reload of the handle from `0x2160` followed by the randomized or zero second argument.
Prototype corrections were part of the matching tactic: changing argument signedness/width in headers helped make generated code line up with the original instructions.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The only human text excerpt besides metadata was a co-author trailer for VetriTheRetri.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or further type `func_80088148`, `func_800237A8`, or `func_80024B94`.
Search for other audio wrappers that store handles in `Fighter::x2160` or nearby fields such as `x2168`.
Check whether item SFX helper parameter order `pan, volume` was later corrected or semantically renamed.
Look for additional matches in `ft_unknown_006.c` around `func_800881D8`, `func_80088478`, and `func_80088510`.
